summary: Sanity/regression backend-only iteration verifying the libmagic defensive-import fix. Backend is UP after the fix: /api/auth/me returns 401 (not 502/504), admin login works, HttpOnly cookie is set, /me returns admin user. Defensive try/except in /app/backend/core/validators.py (ImportError + OSError) is correctly in place; simulated libmagic outage was reproduced in-process and the app degraded gracefully (extension/size checks still block bad files). All primary endpoints from the review request work: contract PDF upload, xlsx inventory import, inventory export, assignments export, assignments export-selected. Full 38-test refactor regression suite + all 9 admin cross-owner focused tests + 10 new libmagic-fix tests ALL PASS. Only pre-existing test-suite friction observed: two tests in the older test_contracts.py file rely on process-level env var REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL (not the .env file) and got hit by 5/min login rate limit — these are TEST issues, not product issues.

minor.issue: Rate limit 5/min occasionally causes test_contracts.py setup fixtures to receive 429 (pre-existing test flake, not a product bug; add sleep between suites or bump limit for tests)

critical_code_review_comments: core/validators.py L14-23 correctly guards `import magic` with `try / except (ImportError, OSError)` and sets `_HAS_MAGIC=False` on failure — matches recommended pattern for optional native deps. Warning is printed to stdout so ops can see when the pod is running in degraded mode.

validate_uploaded_file (L37-49) enforces size + extension guards BEFORE the libmagic branch and short-circuits with `return True` when `_HAS_MAGIC` is False — so upload path is never blocked by a missing native lib. Extension whitelist {.pdf,.xlsx,.xls} is still the primary defense.

Simulated-outage test (in-process ImportError injection) confirms: with libmagic missing, `_HAS_MAGIC=False`, warning printed, and `validate_uploaded_file(b'%PDF fake', 'foo.pdf')` returns cleanly instead of raising. Fix is structurally sound.

SUGGESTION (not blocking): consider replacing `print(...)` with a proper logger.warning() call so warning is captured in structured logs / supervisor logs consistently. Also consider a lightweight /api/healthz endpoint that includes `magic_available: bool` so ops can see degraded state without reading stdout.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_libmagic_defensive_fix.py (NEW - 10 tests: server-health, defensive-import code review, upload endpoints, xlsx import, 3 excel-export endpoints)

success_rate.backend: 100% (10/10 libmagic-fix tests + 38/38 refactor regression + 9/9 admin cross-owner focused = 57/57 relevant tests PASS). Overall test-suite run had 2 pre-existing rate-limit ERRORs in test_contracts.py (test infra issue, not product).

seed_data_creation: None (no persistent seed data added). New tests hit read-only endpoints or use bogus UUIDs that never touch the DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: libmagic defensive-import fix is verified end-to-end. Reusable test file: /app/backend/tests/test_libmagic_defensive_fix.py. It (a) hits the live preview URL to verify server is UP after any future libmagic outage and (b) code-reviews validators.py to catch regressions if someone removes the try/except. NOTE: test_contracts.py (older file) expects REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL env var at pytest-invocation time — export it before running that file. Login is rate-limited 5/min so back-to-back test-suite runs need a 30s sleep or the setup fixtures will 429.
rca_of_the_issue: PREV BUG: libmagic1 native lib disappears from preview pods intermittently (bind-mount/rebuild artifact). python-magic's `import magic` calls `ctypes.CDLL('libmagic.so.1')` at import time and raises OSError if the .so is missing, which propagated through core/validators.py → routes → server.py → worker process crash → 502/504 through Cloudflare. FIX: wrap `import magic` in `try / except (ImportError, OSError)`, set `_HAS_MAGIC=False`, and have validate_uploaded_file early-return after the extension+size guard when the native lib is unavailable. Reproduction (pre-fix): remove libmagic1 pkg, restart backend → worker crash. Verification (post-fix, simulated in-process): inject `ImportError` on magic module load → `_HAS_MAGIC=False`, warning printed, upload validator still runs (extension+size checks only). App survives the outage.
